notes: Aramid (Kevlar 29/49) fabric spall liner. RHAe ~0.2-0.25 per mm vs KE — designed to catch fragments, not stop direct impacts. Kevlar 29 tensile 3.6 GPa, density 1.44 g/cm³. Proven in M113A3 LFTE (ADA267255). ~35% reduction in spall injuries per US DoD studies.

source.reference: DuPont Kevlar technical datasheet, MIL-DTL-62474, US DoD ADA267255
source.methodology: RHAe from ARL ballistic test data on aramid spall liners vs fragments. Tensile strength and density per DuPont Kevlar 29 specification.
